subject: [PATCH v2] mm/list_lru: drain before clearing xarray entry on reparent

reviews.author: Muchun Song
reviews.summary: The reviewer noted that xas_lock_irqsave() in memcg_list_lru_alloc() functions as an RCU read lock, and the patch should not be cleared before reparenting because a concurrent list_lru_del() may corrupt the list if it arrives after xarray clear but before reparenting.
reviews.raw_body:
I see. xas_lock_irqsave() in memcg_list_lru_alloc() functions as an RCU read lock.

Acked-by: Muchun Song <muchun.song@linux.dev>

Thanks.

2026-06-02.patch_summary: This patch fixes a concurrency issue in the list_lru code by draining the per-node lists before clearing the xarray entry on reparenting. The problem occurs when a concurrent thread sees the xarray entry as NULL and walks to the parent, causing corruption of adjacent items' links. To fix this, the order is reversed: each per-node list is reparented and marked dead (LONG_MIN) before the xarray entry is cleared. This ensures that any concurrent list_lru operation will either take the dying memcg's per-node lock or see LONG_MIN and walk to the parent, where the items now live.
